Given RGB images, sensor images, and 3D point clouds, ZSG-IAD generates structured anomaly reports and pixel-level anomaly masks. ZSG-IAD introduces a language-guided two-hop grounding module: (1) anomaly-related sentences select evidence-like latent slots distilled from multimodal features, yielding coarse spatial support; (2) selected slots modulate feature maps via channel-spatial gating and a lightweight decoder to produce fine-grained masks. To improve reliability, we further apply Executable-Rule GRPO with verifiable rewards to promote structured outputs, anomaly-region consistency, and reasoning-conclusion coherence. Experiments across multiple industrial anomaly benchmarks show strong zero-shot performance and more transparent, physically grounded explanations than prior methods. We will release code and annotations to support future research on trustworthy industrial anomaly detection systems.

</details>


### [220] [From Heads to Neurons: Causal Attribution and Steering in Multi-Task Vision-Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.17941)
*Qidong Wang,Junjie Hu,Ming Jiang*

Main category: cs.CV

TL;DR: HONES是一种无需梯度的框架，通过任务相关注意力头条件化FFN神经元的写入贡献来归因和调控多任务VLM中的关键神经元，在四个多模态任务和两个流行VLM上优于现有方法。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有神经元分析通常聚焦单一任务，限制了神经元重要性在不同任务间的可比性；且排序策略孤立地对神经元打分，忽略了任务依赖的信息通路如何塑造FFN神经元的写入效应，这种忽视在多任务设置下加剧了神经元的多义性问题，给识别和干预任务关键神经元引入噪声。

Method: 提出HONES框架：(1) 通过任务相关注意力头条件化，对FFN神经元的因果写入贡献进行无梯度归因；(2) 利用轻量级缩放对显著神经元进行调控，实现任务感知的神经元导向和操控。

Result: 在四个不同的多模态任务和两个流行VLM上，HONES在识别任务关键神经元方面优于现有方法，并在神经元调控后提升了模型性能。

Conclusion: HONES通过注意力头条件化有效解决了多任务VLM中神经元归因的多义性问题，实现了更精确的任务感知神经元识别和干预。

Abstract: Recent work has increasingly explored neuron-level interpretation in vision-language models (VLMs) to identify neurons critical to final predictions. However, existing neuron analyses generally focus on single tasks, limiting the comparability of neuron importance across tasks. Moreover, ranking strategies tend to score neurons in isolation, overlooking how task-dependent information pathways shape the write-in effects of feed-forward network (FFN) neurons. This oversight can exacerbate neuron polysemanticity in multi-task settings, introducing noise into the identification and intervention of task-critical neurons. In this study, we propose HONES (Head-Oriented Neuron Explanation & Steering), a gradient-free framework for task-aware neuron attribution and steering in multi-task VLMs. HONES ranks FFN neurons by their causal write-in contributions conditioned on task-relevant attention heads, and further modulates salient neurons via lightweight scaling. Experiments on four diverse multimodal tasks and two popular VLMs show that HONES outperforms existing methods in identifying task-critical neurons and improves model performance after steering. Our source code is released at: https://github.com/petergit1/HONES.

</details>


### [221] [Brain-Inspired Capture: Evidence-Driven Neuromimetic Perceptual Simulation for Visual Decoding](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.17927)
*Feixue Shao,Guangze Shi,Xueyu Liu,Yongfei Wu,Mingqiang Wei,Jianan Zhang,Jianbo Lu,Guiying Yan,Weihua Yang*

Main category: cs.CV

TL;DR: 本文提出BI-Cap，一种受大脑启发的神经模拟感知仿真范式，通过模拟人类视觉系统的处理机制来弥合神经信号与视觉模态之间的差距，在零样本脑-图像检索任务上分别超越现有最优方法9.2%和8.0%。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有神经信号视觉解码方法受限于神经与视觉模态之间的系统性和随机性差距，且大多忽视了人类视觉系统（HVS）内在的计算机制。

Method: 构建包含四个生物可解释的动态与静态变换的神经模拟管线，结合互信息（MI）引导的动态模糊调节以模拟自适应视觉处理；引入证据驱动的潜在空间表示，显式建模不确定性以缓解神经活动的非平稳性。

Result: 在两个公开基准上的零样本脑-图像检索任务中，BI-Cap分别相对提升9.2%和8.0%，显著优于现有最优方法。

Conclusion: 通过模拟HVS的神经模拟感知范式与不确定性建模，能有效对齐神经与视觉模态，为BCI和计算神经科学提供新思路。

Abstract: Visual decoding of neurophysiological signals is a critical challenge for brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) and computational neuroscience. However, current approaches are often constrained by the systematic and stochastic gaps between neural and visual modalities, largely neglecting the intrinsic computational mechanisms of the Human Visual System (HVS). To address this, we propose Brain-Inspired Capture (BI-Cap), a neuromimetic perceptual simulation paradigm that aligns these modalities by emulating HVS processing. Specifically, we construct a neuromimetic pipeline comprising four biologically plausible dynamic and static transformations, coupled with Mutual Information (MI)-guided dynamic blur regulation to simulate adaptive visual processing. Furthermore, to mitigate the inherent non-stationarity of neural activity, we introduce an evidence-driven latent space representation. This formulation explicitly models uncertainty, thereby ensuring robust neural embeddings. Extensive evaluations on zero-shot brain-to-image retrieval across two public benchmarks demonstrate that BI-Cap substantially outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving relative gains of 9.2\% and 8.0\%, respectively. We have released the source code on GitHub through the link https://github.com/flysnow1024/BI-Cap.

</details>


### [222] [Prompting Foundation Models for Zero-Shot Ship Instance Segmentation in SAR Imagery](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.17920)
*Islam Mansour,Francescopaolo Sica,Michael Schmitt*

Main category: cs.CV

TL;DR: 本文提出一种基于YOLOv11检测器+SAM2通用视觉基础模型的SAR船舶零样本实例分割方法，无需像素级标注即可实现船舶分割，在SSDD数据集上达到0.637 mIoU（全监督基线的89%）和89.2%的检测率。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: SAR图像分析中深度学习受限于像素级标注数据的匮乏，现有方法需要大量人工标注或微调，缺乏可扩展的零样本分割方案。

Method: 使用YOLOv11检测器在公开SAR数据集上训练生成船舶边界框，以此作为空间约束引导SAM2生成实例分割掩码，无需掩码标注、微调或适配器。

Result: 在SSDD基准上mIoU达0.637（全监督基线89%），船舶检测率89.2%，验证了零样本分割的有效性。

Conclusion: 仅靠SAR训练检测器提供的空间约束即可有效规约基础模型预测，缓解光学-SAR域差异，为SAR图像理解提供了一条可扩展、标注高效的路径。

Abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) plays a critical role in maritime surveillance, yet deep learning for SAR analysis is limited by the lack of pixel-level annotations. This paper explores how general-purpose vision foundation models can enable zero-shot ship instance segmentation in SAR imagery, eliminating the need for pixel-level supervision. A YOLOv11-based detector trained on open SAR datasets localizes ships via bounding boxes, which then prompt the Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM2) to produce instance masks without any mask annotations. Unlike prior SAM-based SAR approaches that rely on fine tuning or adapters, our method demonstrates that spatial constraints from a SAR-trained detector alone can effectively regularize foundation model predictions. This design partially mitigates the optical-SAR domain gap and enables downstream applications such as vessel classification, size estimation, and wake analysis. Experiments on the SSDD benchmark achieve a mean IoU of 0.637 (89% of a fully supervised baseline) with an overall ship detection rate of 89.2%, confirming a scalable, annotation-efficient pathway toward foundation-model-driven SAR image understanding.

</details>


### [223] [Beyond Binary Contrast: Modeling Continuous Skeleton Action Spaces with Transitional Anchors](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.17914)
*Yingjie Feng,Yi Wang,Jiaze Wang,Anfeng Liu,Zhuotao Tian*

Main category: cs.CV

TL;DR: TranCLR提出了一种基于过渡锚点的对比学习框架，通过显式建模动作连续性来解决传统对比学习中的特征碎片化问题，在多个骨骼动作识别数据集上取得了最优性能。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有自监督对比学习方法使用二元对比目标，忽略了人体运动的固有连续性，导致特征聚类碎片化且类别边界刚性，无法有效建模动作空间的连续几何结构。

Method: 提出TranCLR框架，包含两个核心模块：(1) Action Transitional Anchor Construction (ATAC) 显式建模过渡状态的几何结构；(2) Multi-Level Geometric Manifold Calibration (MGMC) 在多个连续性级别上自适应校准动作流形，形成更平滑且具判别性的表示空间。

Result: 在NTU RGB+D、NTU RGB+D 120和PKU-MMD三个数据集上，TranCLR取得了优越的识别精度和校准性能，有效学习了连续且具有不确定性感知的骨骼表示。

Conclusion: 通过引入过渡锚点和多层级几何流形校准，TranCLR成功将动作连续性先验融入对比学习，为骨骼动作识别提供了一种更符合人体运动本质的表示学习范式。

Abstract: Self-supervised contrastive learning has emerged as a powerful paradigm for skeleton-based action recognition by enforcing consistency in the embedding space. However, existing methods rely on binary contrastive objectives that overlook the intrinsic continuity of human motion, resulting in fragmented feature clusters and rigid class boundaries. To address these limitations, we propose TranCLR, a Transitional anchor-based Contrastive Learning framework that captures the continuous geometry of the action space. Specifically, the proposed Action Transitional Anchor Construction (ATAC) explicitly models the geometric structure of transitional states to enhance the model's perception of motion continuity. Building upon these anchors, a Multi-Level Geometric Manifold Calibration (MGMC) mechanism is introduced to adaptively calibrate the action manifold across multiple levels of continuity, yielding a smoother and more discriminative representation space. Extensive experiments on the NTU RGB+D, NTU RGB+D 120 and PKU-MMD datasets demonstrate that TranCLR achieves superior accuracy and calibration performance, effectively learning continuous and uncertainty-aware skeleton representations. The code is available at https://github.com/Philchieh/TranCLR.

</details>


### [224] [LARY: A Latent Action Representation Yielding Benchmark for Generalizable Vision-to-Action Alignment](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.11689)
*Dujun Nie,Fengjiao Chen,Qi Lv,Jun Kuang,Xiaoyu Li,Xuezhi Cao,Xunliang Cai*

Main category: cs.CV

TL;DR: 本文提出了LARY基准，用于评估潜在动作表征在高层语义动作和低层机器人控制中的能力。实验发现：通用视觉基础模型（无动作监督）优于专门的潜在动作模型；基于潜在视觉空间比像素空间更接近物理动作空间。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 大规模人类动作视频虽可作为可扩展的无标注数据源，但如何将其中的视觉信号转化为与本体无关的潜在动作表征（latent actions）是一个关键挑战，且目前缺乏对潜在动作表征在机器人控制中能力的严格评估。

Method: 构建LARY统一基准框架，包含超过100万视频（1000小时）、151个动作类别、62万图像对和59.5万运动轨迹，涵盖多种本体和环境，用于评估潜在动作表征在高层语义和低层控制两个层面的表现。

Result: (i) 无任何动作监督训练的通用视觉基础模型，在潜在动作表征上优于专门的具身潜在动作模型；(ii) 基于潜在空间（latent space）的视觉表征与物理动作空间的对齐程度从根本上优于基于像素空间（pixel space）的表征。

Conclusion: 通用视觉表征内在地编码了与物理控制相关的动作知识；语义层面的抽象（semantic-level abstraction）是从视觉到动作比像素级重建更有效的路径。

Abstract: While the shortage of explicit action data limits Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, human action videos offer a scalable yet unlabeled data source. A critical challenge in utilizing large-scale human video datasets lies in transforming visual signals into ontology-independent representations, known as latent actions. However, the capacity of latent action representation to derive robust control from visual observations has yet to be rigorously evaluated. We introduce the Latent Action Representation Yielding (LARY) Benchmark, a unified framework for evaluating latent action representations on both high-level semantic actions (what to do) and low-level robotic control (how to do). The comprehensively curated dataset encompasses over one million videos (1,000 hours) spanning 151 action categories, alongside 620K image pairs and 595K motion trajectories across diverse embodiments and environments. Our experiments reveal two crucial insights: (i) General visual foundation models, trained without any action supervision, consistently outperform specialized embodied latent action models. (ii) Latent-based visual space is fundamentally better aligned to physical action space than pixel-based space. These results suggest that general visual representations inherently encode action-relevant knowledge for physical control, and that semantic-level abstraction serves as a fundamentally more effective pathway from vision to action than pixel-level reconstruction.

</details>


<div id='cs.CL'></div>

# cs.CL [[Back]](#toc)

### [225] [SpeechParaling-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Paralinguistic-Aware Speech Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20842)
*Ruohan Liu,Shukang Yin,Tao Wang,Dong Zhang,Weiji Zhuang,Shuhuai Ren,Ran He,Caifeng Shan,Chaoyou Fu*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: SpeechParaling-Bench：一个用于副语言感知语音生成的综合基准，将特征覆盖从不足50个扩展到100多个细粒度特征，包含1000+英中平行语音查询，通过成对比较管道实现可靠评估，揭示当前LALMs在副语言建模方面的显著局限性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 副语言线索对人机自然交互至关重要，但当前大型音频语言模型（LALMs）的评估存在特征覆盖粗糙和评估主观性强的限制，需要更全面的基准和可靠的评估方法。

Method: 1. 构建SpeechParaling-Bench基准：覆盖100+细粒度副语言特征，包含1000+英中平行语音查询，组织为三个渐进挑战性任务（细粒度控制、话语内变化、上下文感知适应）。2. 开发成对比较评估管道：使用LALM作为评判者，将候选响应与固定基线进行比较，通过相对偏好而非绝对评分来减轻主观性。

Result: 实验显示当前LALMs存在显著局限性：即使是领先的专有模型也难以实现全面的静态控制和动态调制副语言特征；在情境对话中，43.3%的错误源于未能正确解释副语言线索。

Conclusion: 当前LALMs在副语言建模方面存在明显不足，需要更鲁棒的副语言建模方法以实现人类对齐的语音助手。SpeechParaling-Bench为评估和改进副语言感知语音生成提供了重要基准和评估框架。

Abstract: Paralinguistic cues are essential for natural human-computer interaction, yet their evaluation in Large Audio-Language Models (LALMs) remains limited by coarse feature coverage and the inherent subjectivity of assessment. To address these challenges, we introduce SpeechParaling-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark for paralinguistic-aware speech generation. It expands existing coverage from fewer than 50 to over 100 fine-grained features, supported by more than 1,000 English-Chinese parallel speech queries, and is organized into three progressively challenging tasks: fine-grained control, intra-utterance variation, and context-aware adaptation. To enable reliable evaluation, we further develop a pairwise comparison pipeline, in which candidate responses are evaluated against a fixed baseline by an LALM-based judge. By framing evaluation as relative preference rather than absolute scoring, this approach mitigates subjectivity and yields more stable and scalable assessments without costly human annotation. Extensive experiments reveal substantial limitations in current LALMs. Even leading proprietary models struggle with comprehensive static control and dynamic modulation of paralinguistic features, while failure to correctly interpret paralinguistic cues accounts for 43.3% of errors in situational dialogue. These findings underscore the need for more robust paralinguistic modeling toward human-aligned voice assistants.

</details>


### [226] [Convergent Evolution: How Different Language Models Learn Similar Number Representations](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20817)
*Deqing Fu,Tianyi Zhou,Mikhail Belkin,Vatsal Sharan,Robin Jia*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 研究发现语言模型学习数字表示时存在两层特征层次：所有模型都学习到傅里叶域中具有周期T尖峰的特征，但只有部分模型学习到几何可分离特征用于模T线性分类。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 探索语言模型如何表示数字，特别是理解为什么不同模型在学习数字特征时表现出相似性（傅里叶域周期尖峰）但又在几何可分离性上存在差异。

Method: 通过理论证明和实证研究相结合的方法：1) 证明傅里叶域稀疏性是模T几何可分离性的必要但不充分条件；2) 实证研究不同训练条件下模型获得几何可分离特征的情况，考察数据、架构、优化器和分词器的影响。

Result: 1) 所有测试模型（Transformer、线性RNN、LSTM、经典词嵌入）都学习到傅里叶域中具有周期T尖峰的特征；2) 只有部分模型学习到几何可分离特征；3) 模型通过两种途径获得几何可分离特征：从通用语言数据中的互补共现信号（文本-数字共现和跨数字交互），或从多标记（非单标记）加法问题。

Conclusion: 研究揭示了特征学习中的趋同进化现象：多种不同模型从不同的训练信号中学习到相似的特征，数据、架构、优化器和分词器都在决定模型是否获得几何可分离特征中起关键作用。

Abstract: Language models trained on natural text learn to represent numbers using periodic features with dominant periods at $T=2, 5, 10$. In this paper, we identify a two-tiered hierarchy of these features: while Transformers, Linear RNNs, LSTMs, and classical word embeddings trained in different ways all learn features that have period-$T$ spikes in the Fourier domain, only some learn geometrically separable features that can be used to linearly classify a number mod-$T$. To explain this incongruity, we prove that Fourier domain sparsity is necessary but not sufficient for mod-$T$ geometric separability. Empirically, we investigate when model training yields geometrically separable features, finding that the data, architecture, optimizer, and tokenizer all play key roles. In particular, we identify two different routes through which models can acquire geometrically separable features: they can learn them from complementary co-occurrence signals in general language data, including text-number co-occurrence and cross-number interaction, or from multi-token (but not single-token) addition problems. Overall, our results highlight the phenomenon of convergent evolution in feature learning: A diverse range of models learn similar features from different training signals.

</details>


### [227] [Can "AI" Be a Doctor? A Study of Empathy, Readability, and Alignment in Clinical LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20791)
*Mariano Barone,Francesco Di Serio,Roberto Moio,Marco Postiglione,Giuseppe Riccio,Antonio Romano,Vincenzo Moscato*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: LLMs在医疗沟通中与临床标准存在偏差，通过多维度评估发现模型在情感极性、语言复杂度方面与医生存在显著差异，协作重写能最佳对齐临床标准。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: LLMs在医疗领域部署日益增多，但其与临床沟通标准的对齐程度尚未得到充分量化评估，需要系统分析模型在医学解释和医患互动中的表现差异。

Method: 对通用和领域专用LLMs进行多维度评估，包括语义保真度、可读性和情感共鸣分析；采用共情导向提示、协作重写等策略改善模型输出；进行双利益相关者评估（医生和患者）。

Result: 基线模型情感极性比医生更负面（43.14-45.10% vs. 37.25%），语言复杂度更高（FKGL达16.91-17.60 vs. 11.47-12.50）；共情提示降低负面情感和复杂度但不改善语义保真度；协作重写实现最佳对齐（语义相似度达0.93）。

Conclusion: LLMs在医疗沟通中无法超越医生的认知标准，患者更偏好重写版本；模型最适合作为协作沟通增强工具而非临床专业知识的替代品。

Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in healthcare, yet their communicative alignment with clinical standards remains insufficiently quantified. We conduct a multidimensional evaluation of general-purpose and domain-specialized LLMs across structured medical explanations and real-world physician-patient interactions, analyzing semantic fidelity, readability, and affective resonance. Baseline models amplify affective polarity relative to physicians (Very Negative: 43.14-45.10% vs. 37.25%) and, in larger architectures such as GPT-5 and Claude, produce substantially higher linguistic complexity (FKGL up to 16.91-17.60 vs. 11.47-12.50 in physician-authored responses). Empathy-oriented prompting reduces extreme negativity and lowers grade-level complexity (up to -6.87 FKGL points for GPT-5) but does not significantly increase semantic fidelity. Collaborative rewriting yields the strongest overall alignment. Rephrase configurations achieve the highest semantic similarity to physician answers (up to mean = 0.93) while consistently improving readability and reducing affective extremity. Dual stakeholder evaluation shows that no model surpasses physicians on epistemic criteria, whereas patients consistently prefer rewritten variants for clarity and emotional tone. These findings suggest that LLMs function most effectively as collaborative communication enhancers rather than replacements for clinical expertise.

</details>


### [228] [Working Memory Constraints Scaffold Learning in Transformers under Data Scarcity](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20789)
*Pranava Madhyastha,Dagmar Adamcova*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 将人类工作记忆约束整合到Transformer架构中，通过固定宽度窗口和时序衰减注意力机制改进GPT-2，在有限数据下提升语法判断准确性和人类阅读时间对齐度


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 研究动机是将人类认知中的工作记忆约束整合到Transformer架构中，探索认知启发式注意力机制是否能改善语言模型的语法准确性和与人类语言处理的对齐度，特别是在数据有限的情况下

Method: 方法包括：1）在Transformer架构中实现认知启发的注意力变体，包括基于固定宽度窗口和基于时序衰减的注意力机制；2）从头开始训练修改后的GPT-2模型；3）使用发展合理的数据集（1000万和1亿词）进行训练；4）在语法判断任务（BLiMP）和人类阅读时间数据对齐度上进行评估

Result: 结果表明：1）认知启发的约束，特别是固定宽度注意力，能显著提高语法准确性，尤其是在训练数据稀缺时；2）这些受约束的模型倾向于与人类处理指标有更强的对齐度；3）约束作为有益的归纳偏置，引导模型获得更稳健的语言表征

Conclusion: 结论是认知启发的注意力约束可以作为有益的归纳偏置，特别是在数据有限的环境中，引导语言模型获得更稳健的语言表征，并更好地与人类语言处理对齐

Abstract: We investigate the integration of human-like working memory constraints into the Transformer architecture and implement several cognitively inspired attention variants, including fixed-width windows based and temporal decay based attention mechanisms. Our modified GPT-2 models are trained from scratch on developmentally plausible datasets (10M and 100M words). Performance is evaluated on grammatical judgment tasks (BLiMP) and alignment with human reading time data. Our results indicate that these cognitively-inspired constraints, particularly fixed-width attention, can significantly improve grammatical accuracy especially when training data is scarce. These constrained models also tend to show a stronger alignment with human processing metrics. The findings suggest that such constraints may serve as a beneficial inductive bias, guiding models towards more robust linguistic representations, especially in data-limited settings.

</details>


### [229] [Exploiting LLM-as-a-Judge Disposition on Free Text Legal QA via Prompt Optimization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20726)
*Mohamed Hesham Elganayni,Runsheng Chen,Sebastian Nagl,Matthias Grabmair*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 本文研究在LLM作为裁判评估自由文本法律问答时，提示设计和裁判选择的作用，发现自动提示优化优于人工设计，宽松裁判反馈比严格反馈产生更一致且可迁移的优化效果。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 探索在LLM作为裁判评估自由文本法律问答时，提示设计和裁判选择对评估质量的影响，特别是自动提示优化是否优于人工设计，以及不同裁判反馈风格和跨裁判迁移的效果。

Method: 在LEXam基准上，使用ProTeGi方法对任务提示进行优化，采用两个裁判模型（Qwen3-32B和DeepSeek-V3）对四个任务模型提供反馈，然后测试跨裁判迁移效果，分析不同反馈风格（宽松vs严格）的影响。

Result: 自动优化始终优于基线，宽松裁判反馈比严格反馈产生更高且更一致的性能提升。使用宽松反馈优化的提示能更好地迁移到严格裁判，而反向迁移效果较差。分析显示宽松裁判提供更宽容的反馈，产生适用性更广的提示，而严格裁判产生限制性反馈，导致裁判特定的过拟合。

Conclusion: 算法优化提示在训练数据上可以超越人工设计的提示，裁判在优化过程中的倾向性（宽松vs严格）显著影响提示的泛化能力，宽松裁判反馈产生的提示具有更好的跨裁判迁移性。

Abstract: This work explores the role of prompt design and judge selection in LLM-as-a-Judge evaluations of free text legal question answering. We examine whether automatic task prompt optimization improves over human-centered design, whether optimization effectiveness varies by judge feedback style, and whether optimized prompts transfer across judges. We systematically address these questions on the LEXam benchmark by optimizing task prompts using the ProTeGi method with feedback from two judges (Qwen3-32B, DeepSeek-V3) across four task models, and then testing cross-judge transfer. Automatic optimization consistently outperforms the baseline, with lenient judge feedback yielding higher and more consistent gains than strict judge feedback. Prompts optimized with lenient feedback transfer better to strict judges than the reverse direction. Analysis reveals that lenient judges provide permissive feedback, yielding prompts with broader applicability, whereas strict judges produce restrictive feedback, leading to judge-specific overfitting. Our findings demonstrate algorithmically optimizing prompts on training data can outperform human-centered prompt design and that judges' dispositions during optimization shape prompt generalizability. Code and optimized prompts are available at https://github.com/TUMLegalTech/icail2026-llm-judge-gaming.

</details>


### [230] [ORPHEAS: A Cross-Lingual Greek-English Embedding Model for Retrieval-Augmented Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20666)
*Ioannis E. Livieris,Athanasios Koursaris,Alexandra Apostolopoulou,Konstantinos Kanaris Dimitris Tsakalidis,George Domalis*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: ORPHEAS是一个专门针对希腊语-英语双语检索增强生成的嵌入模型，通过知识图谱微调方法在多领域语料上训练，在单语和跨语言检索任务中优于现有多语言嵌入模型。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有多语言嵌入模型在众多语言间分配表示能力，对希腊语的优化有限，无法编码希腊文本的形态复杂性和领域特定术语结构，需要专门的双语嵌入模型来支持希腊语-英语的检索增强生成应用。

Method: 提出ORPHEAS模型，采用基于知识图谱的微调方法生成高质量数据集，在多样化的多领域语料上进行训练，实现语言无关的语义表示。

Result: 在单语和跨语言检索基准测试中，ORPHEAS优于最先进的多语言嵌入模型，表明对形态复杂语言的领域专门化微调不会损害跨语言检索能力。

Conclusion: 专门针对希腊语-英语双语场景的嵌入模型能够更好地处理希腊语的形态复杂性和领域特定术语，同时在跨语言检索任务中保持优异性能，为双语检索增强生成应用提供了有效解决方案。

Abstract: Effective retrieval-augmented generation across bilingual Greek--English applications requires embedding models capable of capturing both domain-specific semantic relationships and cross-lingual semantic alignment. Existing multilingual embedding models distribute their representational capacity across numerous languages, limiting their optimization for Greek and failing to encode the morphological complexity and domain-specific terminological structures inherent in Greek text. In this work, we propose ORPHEAS, a specialized Greek--English embedding model for bilingual retrieval-augmented generation. ORPHEAS is trained with a high quality dataset generated by a knowledge graph-based fine-tuning methodology which is applied to a diverse multi-domain corpus, which enables language-agnostic semantic representations. The numerical experiments across monolingual and cross-lingual retrieval benchmarks reveal that ORPHEAS outperforms state-of-the-art multilingual embedding models, demonstrating that domain-specialized fine-tuning on morphologically complex languages does not compromise cross-lingual retrieval capability.

</details>


### [231] [Toward Cross-Lingual Quality Classifiers for Multilingual Pretraining Data Selection](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20549)
*Yassine Turki,Vinko Sabolčec,Bettina Messmer,Martin Jaggi*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 该研究探讨了在多语言环境下，利用高资源语言的质量标记来辅助低资源语言数据质量过滤的方法，通过跨语言迁移、第三四分位数采样和保留率调优等策略，证明多语言池化在质量分类中的有效性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 随着大语言模型规模扩大，数据质量过滤变得至关重要。但对于许多语言，缺乏足够的高质量本地数据来训练鲁棒的质量分类器。本研究旨在探索质量标记在嵌入空间中的跨语言一致性，使高资源语言能够辅助低资源语言的数据过滤。

Method: 研究评估了多种过滤策略：跨语言迁移、第三四分位数采样（Q3）和保留率调优。使用1B参数模型在103B token上进行训练，比较大规模多语言池化与单语言基线在排名稳定性和聚合准确性方面的表现。

Result: 大规模多语言池化在排名稳定性和聚合准确性方面经常优于单语言基线。对于高资源语言（如法语），聚合归一化准确性提高了1.2%；对于低资源语言，达到或超过单语言基线。但研究发现，仅靠规模不能保证稳定性，对于高资源语言，需要通过Q3采样或保留率调优来优化决策边界。

Conclusion: 质量标记在嵌入空间中表现出跨语言一致性，使得高资源语言能够有效辅助低资源语言的数据质量过滤。多语言池化策略优于单语言方法，但需要适当的边界优化技术（如Q3采样）来充分发挥多语言信号的优势。

Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) scale, data curation has shifted from maximizing volume to optimizing the signal-to-noise ratio by performing quality filtering. However, for many languages, native high quality data is insufficient to train robust quality classifiers. This work investigates the idea that quality markers in embedding space may show cross-lingual consistency, which would allow high-resource languages to subsidize the filtering of low-resource ones. We evaluate various filtering strategies, including cross-lingual transfer, third quartile sampling (Q3), and retention rate tuning. Our results demonstrate that massive multilingual pooling frequently outperforms monolingual baselines in both rank stability and aggregate accuracy for a 1B model trained on 103B tokens, delivering gains for high resource languages (1.2% increase in aggregate normalized accuracy for French) and matching or exceeding monolingual baselines for low-resource languages. However, we find that scale alone does not guarantee stability. Furthermore, for high-resource languages like French, we show that refining the decision boundary through third quartile sampling (Q3) or tuning the retention rate is necessary to fully leverage the multilingual signal.

</details>


### [232] [Enhancing Research Idea Generation through Combinatorial Innovation and Multi-Agent Iterative Search Strategies](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20548)
*Shuai Chen,Chengzhi Zhang*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 提出基于组合创新理论的多智能体迭代规划搜索策略，通过LLM多智能体系统迭代生成、评估和优化研究想法，提高想法多样性和新颖性


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 科学文献快速增长增加了知识筛选成本，现有LLM方法生成的研究想法往往重复且缺乏深度，需要提高研究想法的多样性和新颖性

Method: 结合迭代知识搜索与LLM多智能体系统，采用多智能体迭代规划搜索策略，通过反复交互生成、评估和精炼研究想法

Result: 在自然语言处理领域实验中，该方法在多样性和新颖性方面优于现有基线；与顶级机器学习会议论文想法相比，生成想法质量介于接收和拒绝论文之间

Conclusion: 该框架是支持高质量研究想法生成的有前景方法，源代码和数据集已在GitHub公开，演示版可在Hugging Face访问

Abstract: Scientific progress depends on the continual generation of innovative re-search ideas. However, the rapid growth of scientific literature has greatly increased the cost of knowledge filtering, making it harder for researchers to identify novel directions. Although existing large language model (LLM)-based methods show promise in research idea generation, the ideas they produce are often repetitive and lack depth. To address this issue, this study proposes a multi-agent iterative planning search strategy inspired by com-binatorial innovation theory. The framework combines iterative knowledge search with an LLM-based multi-agent system to generate, evaluate, and re-fine research ideas through repeated interaction, with the goal of improving idea diversity and novelty. Experiments in the natural language processing domain show that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art base-lines in both diversity and novelty. Further comparison with ideas derived from top-tier machine learning conference papers indicates that the quality of the generated ideas falls between that of accepted and rejected papers. These results suggest that the proposed framework is a promising approach for supporting high-quality research idea generation. The source code and dataset used in this paper are publicly available on Github repository: https://github.com/ChenShuai00/MAGenIdeas. The demo is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/cshuai20/MAGenIdeas.

</details>


### [233] [Knowledge Capsules: Structured Nonparametric Memory Units for LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20487)
*Bin Ju,Shenfeng Weng,Danying Zhou,Kunkai Su,Rongkai Xu*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 提出Knowledge Capsules框架，通过结构化非参数记忆单元和外部键值注入机制，将知识直接整合到注意力计算中，相比传统RAG在长上下文和多跳推理中表现更稳定准确。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 传统LLMs将知识编码在参数权重中，更新扩展成本高。RAG通过检索文本扩充上下文，但知识仅作为注意力机制中的竞争性token存在，影响间接且不稳定，尤其在长上下文和多跳推理场景中表现不佳。

Method: 提出Knowledge Capsules结构化非参数记忆单元，表示规范化关系知识，可直接从文档语料库构建。引入External Key Value Injection框架，将capsules编译为注意力兼容的键值表示，使外部知识直接参与模型的注意力计算，将知识整合从上下文级提升到内存级交互。

Result: 在多个QA基准测试中，该框架持续优于RAG和GraphRAG，在长上下文和多跳推理中表现出改进的稳定性和准确性，且无需参数更新。

Conclusion: Knowledge Capsules和KVI框架通过将外部知识直接整合到注意力计算中，实现了更稳定准确的知识利用，为LLMs的知识更新和扩展提供了有效解决方案，无需模型参数更新。

Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) encode knowledge in parametric weights, making it costly to update or extend without retraining. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) mitigates this limitation by appending retrieved text to the input, but operates purely through context expansion, where external knowledge competes as tokens within the attention mechanism. As a result, its influence is indirect and often unstable, particularly in long context and multi hop reasoning scenarios. We propose Knowledge Capsules, structured nonparametric memory units that represent normalized relational knowledge and can be constructed directly from document corpora using a frozen base model. Instead of injecting knowledge as text, we introduce an External Key Value Injection (KVI) framework that compiles capsules into attention-compatible key value representations, enabling external knowledge to directly participate in the model's attention computation. By shifting knowledge integration from context-level augmentation to memory level interaction, the proposed framework consistently outperforms RAG and GraphRAG across multiple QA benchmarks, with improved stability and accuracy in long context and multi hop reasoning, while requiring no parameter updates.

</details>


### [234] [DialToM: A Theory of Mind Benchmark for Forecasting State-Driven Dialogue Trajectories](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20443)
*Neemesh Yadav,Palakorn Achananuparp,Jing Jiang,Ee-Peng Lim*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: DialToM是一个基于人类对话构建的ToM基准测试，评估LLMs不仅能否预测心理状态（Literal ToM），还能否利用这些状态进行前瞻性诊断预测（Functional ToM），揭示了推理不对称性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 尽管LLMs表现出心智理论能力，但尚不清楚这是源于稳健推理还是虚假相关性。需要构建更自然的评估基准来区分这两种可能性。

Method: 构建DialToM基准：基于自然人类对话的多选框架，评估Literal ToM（心理状态预测）和Functional ToM（通过前瞻性诊断预测评估心理状态的功能效用）。

Result: 发现显著的推理不对称性：LLMs擅长识别心理状态，但大多数（除Gemini 3 Pro外）无法利用这种理解来预测社会轨迹。人类与LLM生成的推理之间仅有弱语义相似性。

Conclusion: LLMs的心智理论能力存在局限性，特别是在功能应用方面。DialToM基准为评估ToM提供了更全面的框架，有助于推动更稳健的推理能力发展。

Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been shown to possess Theory of Mind (ToM) abilities. However, it remains unclear whether this stems from robust reasoning or spurious correlations. We introduce DialToM, a human-verified benchmark built from natural human dialogue using a multiple-choice framework. We evaluate not only mental state prediction (Literal ToM) but also the functional utility of these states (Functional ToM) through Prospective Diagnostic Forecasting -- probing whether models can identify state-consistent dialogue trajectories solely from mental-state profiles. Our results reveal a significant reasoning asymmetry: while LLMs excel at identifying mental states, most (except for Gemini 3 Pro) fail to leverage this understanding to forecast social trajectories. Additionally, we find only weak semantic similarities between human and LLM-generated inferences. To facilitate reproducibility, the DialToM dataset and evaluation code are publicly available at https://github.com/Stealth-py/DialToM.

</details>


### [235] [Hybrid Policy Distillation for LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20244)
*Wenhong Zhu,Ruobing Xie,Rui Wang,Pengfei Liu*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 提出混合策略蒸馏(HPD)，通过整合前向和反向KL散度的优势，结合离策略数据和轻量级近似在策略采样，改进大语言模型的知识蒸馏效果。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有知识蒸馏方法在散度方向、优化策略和数据机制方面的选择相互交织，缺乏统一视角。需要一种能平衡模式覆盖和模式寻求，同时兼顾计算效率和优化稳定性的蒸馏方法。

Method: 首先将知识蒸馏重新表述为标记级别的加权对数似然目标，建立统一视图。然后提出混合策略蒸馏(HPD)，整合前向KL（模式覆盖）和反向KL（模式寻求）的互补优势，并组合离策略数据与轻量级近似在策略采样。

Result: 在长生成数学推理以及短生成对话和代码任务上验证HPD，展示了改进的优化稳定性、计算效率和最终性能，适用于不同模型系列和规模。

Conclusion: HPD通过统一的知识蒸馏框架，有效平衡了模式覆盖与模式寻求，结合不同数据机制，为大规模语言模型压缩提供了高效稳定的解决方案。

Abstract: Knowledge distillation (KD) is a powerful paradigm for compressing large language models (LLMs), whose effectiveness depends on intertwined choices of divergence direction, optimization strategy, and data regime. We break down the design of existing KD methods and present a unified view that establishes connections between them, reformulating KD as a reweighted log-likelihood objective at the token level. We further propose Hybrid Policy Distillation (HPD), which integrates the complementary advantages of forward and reverse KL to balance mode coverage and mode-seeking, and combines off-policy data with lightweight, approximate on-policy sampling. We validate HPD on long-generation math reasoning as well as short-generation dialogue and code tasks, demonstrating improved optimization stability, computational efficiency, and final performance across diverse model families and scales. The code related to this work is available at https://github.com/zwhong714/Hybrid-Policy-Distillation.

</details>


### [236] [RADS: Reinforcement Learning-Based Sample Selection Improves Transfer Learning in Low-resource and Imbalanced Clinical Settings](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20256)
*Wei Han,David Martinez,Anna Khanina,Lawrence Cavedon,Karin Verspoor*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: RADS：基于强化学习的自适应领域采样策略，用于极端低资源和不平衡条件下的样本选择，提升模型可迁移性


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 在迁移学习中，少样本微调的成功高度依赖于训练样本的质量。传统主动学习方法（如不确定性采样和多样性采样）在极端低资源和类别不平衡条件下，往往选择异常值而非真正信息丰富的样本，导致性能下降。

Method: 提出RADS（强化自适应领域采样），一种基于强化学习的鲁棒样本选择策略，使用RL识别最具信息量的样本。

Result: 在多个真实世界临床数据集上的实验评估表明，该样本选择策略在保持极端类别不平衡下鲁棒性能的同时，增强了模型的可迁移性，优于传统方法。

Conclusion: RADS为极端低资源和不平衡条件下的迁移学习提供了一种有效的样本选择方案，能够识别真正信息丰富的样本而非异常值，提升模型性能。

Abstract: A common strategy in transfer learning is few shot fine-tuning, but its success is highly dependent on the quality of samples selected as training examples. Active learning methods such as uncertainty sampling and diversity sampling can select useful samples. However, under extremely low-resource and class-imbalanced conditions, they often favor outliers rather than truly informative samples, resulting in degraded performance. In this paper, we introduce RADS (Reinforcement Adaptive Domain Sampling), a robust sample selection strategy using reinforcement learning (RL) to identify the most informative samples. Experimental evaluations on several real world clinical datasets show our sample selection strategy enhances model transferability while maintaining robust performance under extreme class imbalance compared to traditional methods.

</details>


### [237] [Chat2Workflow: A Benchmark for Generating Executable Visual Workflows with Natural Language](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19667)
*Yi Zhong,Buqiang Xu,Yijun Wang,Zifei Shan,Shuofei Qiao,Guozhou Zheng,Ningyu Zhang*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: Chat2Workflow：一个从自然语言生成可执行视觉工作流的基准测试，旨在研究大语言模型能否自动化多轮交互过程，减少人工构建工作流的成本。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 当前工业部署中，可执行视觉工作流已成为主流范式，但完全依赖人工构建，开发成本高、耗时且易出错。研究大语言模型能否自动化这一多轮交互过程。

Method: 提出Chat2Workflow基准，基于真实业务工作流构建，确保生成的工作流可直接部署到Dify、Coze等平台。同时提出鲁棒的智能体框架来缓解循环执行错误。

Result: 实验表明，最先进的语言模型能捕捉高层意图，但难以生成正确、稳定、可执行的工作流，尤其在复杂或需求变化时。提出的智能体框架带来最高5.34%的解决率提升。

Conclusion: Chat2Workflow为推进工业级自动化提供了基础，尽管智能体框架有所改进，但现实差距依然存在，表明该领域仍有重要研究空间。

Abstract: At present, executable visual workflows have emerged as a mainstream paradigm in real-world industrial deployments, offering strong reliability and controllability. However, in current practice, such workflows are almost entirely constructed through manual engineering: developers must carefully design workflows, write prompts for each step, and repeatedly revise the logic as requirements evolve-making development costly, time-consuming, and error-prone. To study whether large language models can automate this multi-round interaction process, we introduce Chat2Workflow, a benchmark for generating executable visual workflows directly from natural language, and propose a robust agentic framework to mitigate recurrent execution errors. Chat2Workflow is built from a large collection of real-world business workflows, with each instance designed so that the generated workflow can be transformed and directly deployed to practical workflow platforms such as Dify and Coze. Experimental results show that while state-of-the-art language models can often capture high-level intent, they struggle to generate correct, stable, and executable workflows, especially under complex or changing requirements. Although our agentic framework yields up to 5.34% resolve rate gains, the remaining real-world gap positions Chat2Workflow as a foundation for advancing industrial-grade automation. Code is available at https://github.com/zjunlp/Chat2Workflow.

</details>


### [238] [TriEx: A Game-based Tri-View Framework for Explaining Internal Reasoning in Multi-Agent LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20043)
*Ziyi Wang,Chen Zhang,Wenjun Peng,Qi Wu,Xinyu Wang*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: TriEx是一个三视图可解释性框架，为LLM智能体在交互式部分可观测环境中的决策提供结构化解释，包含第一人称自我推理、第二人称对手信念状态和第三人称环境审计三个对齐的视角。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 大型语言模型智能体在交互式、部分可观测环境中的可解释性特别具有挑战性，因为决策依赖于不断演变的信念和其他智能体。现有解释通常是自由形式的叙述，难以比较和验证。

Method: 提出TriEx三视图可解释性框架，包含三个对齐的构件：(1) 与行动绑定的结构化第一人称自我推理；(2) 随时间更新的显式第二人称对手信念状态；(3) 基于环境衍生参考信号的第三人称审计。该设计将解释从自由叙述转变为可跨时间和视角比较、检查的证据锚定对象。

Result: 在不完美信息策略游戏的受控测试环境中，TriEx能够对解释忠实度、信念动态和评估者可靠性进行可扩展分析，揭示了智能体所说、所信和所做之间的系统性不匹配。

Conclusion: 可解释性是依赖于交互的属性，需要多视角、证据基础化的评估方法。TriEx框架为LLM智能体提供了结构化、可验证的解释机制，有助于理解智能体在复杂交互环境中的决策过程。

Abstract: Explainability for Large Language Model (LLM) agents is especially challenging in interactive, partially observable settings, where decisions depend on evolving beliefs and other agents. We present \textbf{TriEx}, a tri-view explainability framework that instruments sequential decision making with aligned artifacts: (i) structured first-person self-reasoning bound to an action, (ii) explicit second-person belief states about opponents updated over time, and (iii) third-person oracle audits grounded in environment-derived reference signals. This design turns explanations from free-form narratives into evidence-anchored objects that can be compared and checked across time and perspectives. Using imperfect-information strategic games as a controlled testbed, we show that TriEx enables scalable analysis of explanation faithfulness, belief dynamics, and evaluator reliability, revealing systematic mismatches between what agents say, what they believe, and what they do. Our results highlight explainability as an interaction-dependent property and motivate multi-view, evidence-grounded evaluation for LLM agents. Code is available at https://github.com/Einsam1819/TriEx.

</details>


### [239] [Seeing Isn't Believing: Mitigating Belief Inertia via Active Intervention in Embodied Agents](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.17252)
*Hanlin Wang,Chak Tou Leong,Jian Wang,Wenjie Li*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 论文提出EVU机制解决LLM智能体在具身任务中的信念惯性问题，通过预测-验证-更新流程主动管理信念，显著提升任务成功率。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有LLM智能体在复杂具身任务中常做出次优决策和无效动作，主要原因是忽视了与环境反馈不符的内部信念，即存在"信念惯性"问题。智能体固执地坚持先验信念，即使有明确的观察证据也不更新。

Method: 提出Estimate-Verify-Update (EVU)机制：1) Estimate：预测期望结果；2) Verify：通过显式推理验证预测与观察的一致性；3) Update：基于验证证据主动更新先验信念。EVU生成显式文本信念状态，可作为统一干预机制集成到基于提示和基于训练的智能体推理方法中。

Result: 在三个具身基准测试上的广泛实验表明，EVU持续带来任务成功率的大幅提升。进一步分析验证了该方法能有效缓解信念惯性问题。

Conclusion: EVU机制通过主动信念干预解决了LLM智能体的信念惯性问题，推动了更鲁棒具身智能体的发展。该方法可作为通用框架集成到多种智能体架构中。

Abstract: Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs) have enabled agents to tackle complex embodied tasks through environmental interaction. However, these agents still make suboptimal decisions and perform ineffective actions, as they often overlook critical environmental feedback that differs from their internal beliefs. Through a formal probing analysis, we characterize this as belief inertia, a phenomenon where agents stubbornly adhere to prior beliefs despite explicit observations. To address this, we advocate active belief intervention, moving from passive understanding to active management. We introduce the Estimate-Verify-Update (EVU) mechanism, which empowers agents to predict expected outcomes, verify them against observations through explicit reasoning, and actively update prior beliefs based on the verification evidence. EVU is designed as a unified intervention mechanism that generates textual belief states explicitly, and can be integrated into both prompting-based and training-based agent reasoning methods. Extensive experiments across three embodied benchmarks demonstrate that EVU consistently yields substantial gains in task success rates. Further analyses validate that our approach effectively mitigates belief inertia, advancing the development of more robust embodied agents. Our code is available at https://github.com/WangHanLinHenry/EVU.

</details>


### [240] [Depression Risk Assessment in Social Media via Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19887)
*Giorgia Gulino,Manuel Petrucci*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 基于大语言模型（LLMs）的Reddit帖子抑郁症风险评估系统，通过八种抑郁相关情绪的多标签分类和加权严重性指数计算，在零样本设置下达到与专用微调模型竞争的性能，并成功应用于大规模社交媒体数据分析。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 抑郁症是全球最常见且致残性最强的心理健康问题之一，经常被漏诊和治疗不足。社交媒体平台提供了丰富的自然语言信号，可用于自动化心理健康的监测。本研究旨在利用大语言模型开发一个成本效益高、可扩展的抑郁症风险评估系统。

Method: 提出基于大语言模型的系统，通过多标签分类识别八种与抑郁相关的情绪，并计算加权严重性指数。在DepressionEmo数据集（约6,000个帖子）上进行零样本评估，并在2024-2025年期间从四个Reddit子版块收集的469,692条评论中进行实际应用分析。

Result: 最佳模型gemma3:27b在零样本设置下达到micro-F1=0.75和macro-F1=0.70，与专用微调模型BART（micro-F1=0.80，macro-F1=0.76）竞争性结果。实际应用分析显示不同社区间存在一致且时间稳定的风险特征，r/depression和r/anxiety子版块之间存在明显差异。

Conclusion: 研究证明了基于大语言模型的成本效益高、可扩展方法在大规模心理监测中的可行性，为自动化心理健康评估提供了有效工具。

Abstract: Depression is one of the most prevalent and debilitating mental health conditions worldwide, frequently underdiagnosed and undertreated. The proliferation of social media platforms provides a rich source of naturalistic linguistic signals for the automated monitoring of psychological well-being. In this work, we propose a system based on Large Language Models (LLMs) for depression risk assessment in Reddit posts, through multi-label classification of eight depression-associated emotions and the computation of a weighted severity index. The method is evaluated in a zero-shot setting on the annotated DepressionEmo dataset (~6,000 posts) and applied in-the-wild to 469,692 comments collected from four subreddits over the period 2024-2025. Our best model, gemma3:27b, achieves micro-F1 = 0.75 and macro-F1 = 0.70, results competitive with purpose-built fine-tuned models (BART: micro-F1 = 0.80, macro-F1 = 0.76). The in-the-wild analysis reveals consistent and temporally stable risk profiles across communities, with marked differences between r/depression and r/anxiety. Our findings demonstrate the feasibility of a cost-effective, scalable approach for large-scale psychological monitoring.

</details>


### [241] [From Signal Degradation to Computation Collapse: Uncovering the Two Failure Modes of LLM Quantization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19884)
*Chenxi Zhou,Pengfei Cao,Jiang Li,Bohan Yu,Jinyu Ye,Jun Zhao,Kang Liu*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 论文系统分析了LLM后训练量化中的两种失败模式：信号退化和计算崩溃，并提出了机制感知的干预方法


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 虽然4位量化被认为是理想权衡，但2位量化通常会导致性能灾难性下降。目前不清楚这两种情况的底层机制是否根本不同，因此需要系统性的机制分析来理解量化失败的本质

Method: 进行系统性机制分析，识别两种不同的失败模式：信号退化（计算模式完整但信息精度受损）和计算崩溃（关键组件失效）。基于诊断结果，实施机制感知的干预措施，包括针对性的免训练修复方法

Result: 研究发现：1）信号退化可以通过有针对性的免训练修复来缓解；2）计算崩溃无法通过简单的补偿方法解决，需要结构重建；3）提供了系统性的PTQ失败诊断框架

Conclusion: 量化失败存在两种本质不同的机制，需要不同的解决方案。信号退化可以通过补偿修复，而计算崩溃需要结构重建。这为PTQ提供了系统性的诊断框架，并指导未来的量化方法设计

Abstract: Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) is critical for the efficient deployment of Large Language Models (LLMs). While 4-bit quantization is widely regarded as an optimal trade-off, reducing the precision to 2-bit usually triggers a catastrophic ``performance cliff.'' It remains unclear whether the underlying mechanisms differ fundamentally. Consequently, we conduct a systematic mechanistic analysis, revealing two qualitatively distinct failure modes: Signal Degradation, where the computational patterns remain intact but information precision is impaired by cumulative error; and Computation Collapse, where key components fail to function, preventing correct information processing and destroying the signal in the early layers. Guided by this diagnosis, we conduct mechanism-aware interventions, demonstrating that targeted, training-free repair can mitigate Signal Degradation, but remains ineffective for Computation Collapse. Our findings provide a systematic diagnostic framework for PTQ failures and suggest that addressing Computation Collapse requires structural reconstruction rather than mere compensation.

</details>


### [242] [Bootstrapping Post-training Signals for Open-ended Tasks via Rubric-based Self-play on Pre-training Text](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20051)
*Chengyu Huang,Sheng-Yen Chou,Zhengxin Zhang,Claire Cardie*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: POP是一个自博弈框架，使用相同LLM为每个示例合成评估标准和输入-输出对，通过基于内容的预训练语料库减少奖励黑客和模式崩溃，在多种开放任务上提升性能


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 自博弈作为训练LLM的有前景范式，能最小化监督成本，但现有工作仅适用于可验证任务（如数学和编程）。本文旨在将其扩展到更现实的开放任务，解决传统方法需要昂贵人工或专有模型生成高质量输入-输出对的问题。

Method: 提出POP自博弈框架：1）使用相同LLM为每个示例合成评估标准和输入-输出对；2）基于内容丰富的预训练语料库进行框架基础化，确保生成-验证差距以减少奖励黑客，并防止模式崩溃；3）使用标准评估输出并训练模型。

Result: 在Qwen-2.5-7B模型上，POP提高了预训练和指令调优模型的性能，涵盖从长篇医疗问答到创意写作和指令遵循等多种任务。

Conclusion: POP成功将自博弈扩展到开放任务，通过合成评估标准和基于内容的预训练语料库基础化，有效减少了奖励黑客和模式崩溃，为LLM训练提供了高效且成本低廉的解决方案。

Abstract: Self-play has recently emerged as a promising paradigm to train Large Language Models (LLMs). In self-play, the target LLM creates the task input (e.g., ask a question), which it then addresses itself by producing a task output (e.g., give an answer). A reward model evaluates the output, and the rewards are then used to train the LLM, typically via Reinforcement Learning (RL). Self-play incurs minimal supervision costs, and this is especially helpful for post-training LLMs, which require high-quality input-output pairs that traditionally have to be written by humans or expensive proprietary models. However, existing work explores self-play only for verifiable tasks such as math and coding. Instead, we seek to extend it to more realistic open-ended tasks. In particular, we propose POP, a self-play framework that uses the same LLM to synthesize evaluation rubrics, along with input-output pairs, for each example. The rubric is then used to evaluate outputs and train the model. We further ground the framework on a content-rich pretraining corpus to (1) ensure a generation-verification gap and reduce reward hacking, and (2) prevent mode collapse. On Qwen-2.5-7B, POP increases performance of both pretrained and instruction-tuned models, across different tasks ranging from long-form Healthcare QA to creative writing and instruction following.

</details>


### [243] [Cross-Model Consistency of AI-Generated Exercise Prescriptions: A Repeated Generation Study Across Three Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19598)
*Kihyuk Lee*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 研究比较了GPT-4.1、Claude Sonnet 4.6和Gemini 2.5 Flash三个大语言模型在温度=0条件下运动处方输出的重复生成一致性，发现语义相似度得分掩盖了根本不同的生成行为模式。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 评估不同LLM在临床运动处方生成任务中的输出一致性，揭示单次输出评估无法捕捉的模型行为差异，为临床部署提供决策依据。

Method: 在温度=0条件下，让三个LLM（GPT-4.1、Claude Sonnet 4.6、Gemini 2.5 Flash）为六个临床场景各生成20次运动处方，共360个输出，从语义相似度、输出可重现性、FITT分类和安全表达四个维度进行分析。

Result: GPT-4.1语义相似度最高（0.955）且100%输出唯一，Gemini 2.5 Flash相似度0.950但仅有27.5%唯一输出（依赖文本重复），Claude Sonnet 4.6相似度最低（0.903）。安全表达在所有模型中均达到天花板水平，缺乏区分度。

Conclusion: 相同解码设置下不同LLM表现出根本不同的输出一致性模式，模型选择应是临床决策而非纯技术选择，重复生成条件下的输出行为应作为LLM运动处方系统可靠部署的核心标准。

Abstract: This study compared repeated generation consistency of exercise prescription outputs across three large language models (LLMs), specifically GPT-4.1, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and Gemini 2.5 Flash, under temperature=0 conditions. Each model generated prescriptions for six clinical scenarios 20 times, yielding 360 total outputs analyzed across four dimensions: semantic similarity, output reproducibility, FITT classification, and safety expression. Mean semantic similarity was highest for GPT-4.1 (0.955), followed by Gemini 2.5 Flash (0.950) and Claude Sonnet 4.6 (0.903), with significant inter-model differences confirmed (H = 458.41, p < .001). Critically, these scores reflected fundamentally different generative behaviors: GPT-4.1 produced entirely unique outputs (100%) with stable semantic content, while Gemini 2.5 Flash showed pronounced output repetition (27.5% unique outputs), indicating that its high similarity score derived from text duplication rather than consistent reasoning. Identical decoding settings thus yielded fundamentally different consistency profiles, a distinction that single-output evaluations cannot capture. Safety expression reached ceiling levels across all models, confirming its limited utility as a differentiating metric. These results indicate that model selection constitutes a clinical rather than merely technical decision, and that output behavior under repeated generation conditions should be treated as a core criterion for reliable deployment of LLM-based exercise prescription systems.

</details>


### [244] [Impact of large language models on peer review opinions from a fine-grained perspective: Evidence from top conference proceedings in AI](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19578)
*Wenqing Wu,Chengzhi Zhang,Yi Zhao,Tong Bao*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 该研究分析了大型语言模型（LLMs）出现后对学术同行评审报告的影响，发现评审文本变得更长、更流畅，更注重摘要和表面清晰度，但深层评估维度（如原创性、可重复性）的关注度下降。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 随着LLMs的快速发展，学术界面临前所未有的冲击，特别是在学术交流领域。虽然先前研究表明LLMs开始影响同行评审，但尚不清楚它们是否改变了评审的核心评估功能，以及LLMs如何影响评审报告的语言形式、评估重点和推荐相关信号。

Method: 研究从细粒度层面分析LLMs出现前后学术论文同行评审报告的变化：1）分析评审意见的语言特征（长度、复杂度）；2）自动标注单个评审句子的评估方面；3）使用最大似然估计方法识别可能由LLMs修改或生成的评审报告；4）评估LLM辅助评审报告中提到的评估方面对论文决策推荐信息性的影响。

Result: 结果显示：1）LLMs出现后，同行评审文本变得更长、更流畅；2）更强调摘要和表面清晰度，语言模式更加标准化（特别是自信心较低的评审者）；3）对深层评估维度（如原创性、可重复性、细微批判性推理）的关注度下降。

Conclusion: LLMs正在改变同行评审的性质，使其更注重表面特征和标准化表达，但可能以牺牲对学术工作核心质量（如原创性、严谨性）的深入评估为代价。这引发了关于LLMs在学术评估中适当角色以及如何保持评审深度和质量的重要问题。

Abstract: With the rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs), the academic community has faced unprecedented disruptions, particularly in the realm of academic communication. The primary function of peer review is improving the quality of academic manuscripts, such as clarity, originality and other evaluation aspects. Although prior studies suggest that LLMs are beginning to influence peer review, it remains unclear whether they are altering its core evaluative functions. Moreover, the extent to which LLMs affect the linguistic form, evaluative focus, and recommendation-related signals of peer-review reports has yet to be systematically examined. In this study, we examine the changes in peer review reports for academic articles following the emergence of LLMs, emphasizing variations at fine-grained level. Specifically, we investigate linguistic features such as the length and complexity of words and sentences in review comments, while also automatically annotating the evaluation aspects of individual review sentences. We also use a maximum likelihood estimation method, previously established, to identify review reports that potentially have modified or generated by LLMs. Finally, we assess the impact of evaluation aspects mentioned in LLM-assisted review reports on the informativeness of recommendation for paper decision-making. The results indicate that following the emergence of LLMs, peer review texts have become longer and more fluent, with increased emphasis on summaries and surface-level clarity, as well as more standardized linguistic patterns, particularly reviewers with lower confidence score. At the same time, attention to deeper evaluative dimensions, such as originality, replicability, and nuanced critical reasoning, has declined.

</details>


### [245] [Detecting Hallucinations in SpeechLLMs at Inference Time Using Attention Maps](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19565)
*Jonas Waldendorf,Bashar Awwad Shiekh Hasan,Evgenii Tsymbalov*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 该论文提出了一种基于注意力模式的语音大语言模型幻觉检测方法，使用四种注意力衍生指标训练轻量级分类器，无需黄金标准输出，在ASR和语音翻译任务中优于现有基线方法。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 语音大语言模型中的幻觉问题存在显著风险，但现有检测方法通常依赖昂贵或不切实际的黄金标准输出。此外，为文本LLM开发的幻觉检测方法无法直接捕捉音频特定信号，因此需要开发专门针对语音LLM的检测方法。

Method: 提出四种注意力衍生指标：AUDIORATIO、AUDIOCONSISTENCY、AUDIOENTROPY和TEXTENTROPY，旨在捕捉与幻觉相关的病态注意力模式。基于这些特征训练轻量级逻辑回归分类器，实现高效的推理时检测。研究还探索了使用约100个注意力头而非全部头来改善泛化性能。

Result: 在Qwen-2-Audio和Voxtral-3B模型上的自动语音识别和语音到文本翻译任务评估显示，该方法在域内数据上优于基于不确定性和先前注意力方法的基线，PR-AUC提升高达+0.23，并能泛化到域外ASR设置。使用约100个注意力头相比使用全部头能改善域外泛化性能。

Conclusion: 虽然方法的有效性依赖于具体模型且需要任务特定训练，但研究结果表明注意力模式为语音大语言模型的幻觉检测提供了有价值的工具，特别是在缺乏黄金标准输出的情况下。

Abstract: Hallucinations in Speech Large Language Models (SpeechLLMs) pose significant risks, yet existing detection methods typically rely on gold-standard outputs that are costly or impractical to obtain. Moreover, hallucination detection methods developed for text-based LLMs do not directly capture audio-specific signals. We investigate four attention-derived metrics: AUDIORATIO, AUDIOCONSISTENCY, AUDIOENTROPY, and TEXTENTROPY, designed to capture pathological attention patterns associated with hallucination, and train lightweight logistic regression classifiers on these features for efficient inference-time detection. Across automatic speech recognition and speech-to-text translation tasks, evaluations on Qwen-2-Audio and Voxtral-3B show that our approach outperforms uncertainty-based and prior attention-based baselines on in-domain data, achieving improvements of up to +0.23 PR-AUC, and generalises to out-of-domain ASR settings. We further find that strong performance can be achieved with approximately 100 attention heads, improving out-of-domain generalisation compared to using all heads. While effectiveness is model-dependent and task-specific training is required, our results demonstrate that attention patterns provide a valuable tool for hallucination detection in SpeechLLMs.

</details>


### [246] [Taming Actor-Observer Asymmetry in Agents via Dialectical Alignment](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19548)
*Bobo Li,Rui Wu,Zibo Ji,Meishan Zhang,Hao Fei,Min Zhang,Mong-Li Lee,Wynne Hsu*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 论文发现LLM智能体在角色扮演中会产生类似人类的认知偏差（行动者-观察者不对称），并提出ReTAS方法通过辩证对齐来缓解这种偏差


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 多智能体框架通过角色专业化增强了可靠性，但同时也引入了人类认知偏差——行动者-观察者不对称（AOA），即行动者倾向于将失败归因于外部因素，而观察者则将相同错误归因于内部缺陷

Method: 提出ReTAS（Reasoning via Thesis-Antithesis-Synthesis）方法，通过辩证对齐训练模型，结合辩证思维链和群体相对策略优化，引导智能体将冲突观点合成为客观共识

Result: 使用新的模糊失败基准测试发现，仅交换视角就能在超过20%的情况下触发AOA效应；ReTAS有效缓解了归因不一致性，并在模糊场景中显著提高了故障解决率

Conclusion: 角色专业化虽然有益，但会引入认知偏差；通过辩证对齐方法如ReTAS，可以引导LLM智能体实现视角不变推理，从而提高多智能体系统的可靠性和客观性

Abstract: Large Language Model agents have rapidly evolved from static text generators into dynamic systems capable of executing complex autonomous workflows. To enhance reliability, multi-agent frameworks assigning specialized roles are increasingly adopted to enable self-reflection and mutual auditing. While such role-playing effectively leverages domain expert knowledge, we find it simultaneously induces a human-like cognitive bias known as Actor-Observer Asymmetry (AOA). Specifically, an agent acting as an actor (during self-reflection) tends to attribute failures to external factors, whereas an observer (during mutual auditing) attributes the same errors to internal faults. We quantify this using our new Ambiguous Failure Benchmark, which reveals that simply swapping perspectives triggers the AOA effect in over 20% of cases for most models. To tame this bias, we introduce ReTAS (Reasoning via Thesis-Antithesis-Synthesis), a model trained through dialectical alignment to enforce perspective-invariant reasoning. By integrating dialectical chain-of-thought with Group Relative Policy Optimization, ReTAS guides agents to synthesize conflicting viewpoints into an objective consensus. Experiments demonstrate that ReTAS effectively mitigates attribution inconsistency and significantly improves fault resolution rates in ambiguous scenarios.

</details>


### [247] [LePREC: Reasoning as Classification over Structured Factors for Assessing Relevance of Legal Issues](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19464)
*Fanyu Wang,Xiaoxi Kang,Paul Burgess,Aashish Srivastava,Chetan Arora,Adnan Trakic,Lay-Ki Soon,Md Khalid Hossain,Lizhen Qu*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 本文提出LePREC框架，结合神经生成与符号统计推理，解决LLM在法律问题识别中的精度不足问题，在马来西亚合同法案例数据集上实现30-40%的性能提升。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 全球超过一半人口因法律资源有限而难以满足司法需求。虽然大语言模型展现出强大的推理能力，但在法律问题识别这一基础任务上仍面临挑战，GPT-4o的精度仅为62%，无法满足实际需求。

Method: 提出LePREC（法律专业启发式推理激发与分类）神经符号框架：1）神经组件利用LLM将法律描述转换为代表不同分析因素的问答对；2）符号组件在这些离散特征上应用稀疏线性模型，学习显式代数权重以识别最具信息量的推理因素。该框架通过透明特征加权实现可解释性，并通过基于相关的统计分类保持数据效率。

Result: 在769个马来西亚合同法真实法庭案例数据集上的实验表明，LePREC相比GPT-4o、Claude等先进LLM基线实现了30-40%的性能提升。基于相关的因素-问题分析为相关性决策提供了更数据高效的解决方案。

Conclusion: 神经符号方法LePREC通过结合神经生成与结构化统计推理，显著提升了法律问题识别的精度和可解释性，为解决法律资源有限问题提供了有效技术方案。

Abstract: More than half of the global population struggles to meet their civil justice needs due to limited legal resources. While Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive reasoning capabilities, significant challenges remain even at the foundational step of legal issue identification. To investigate LLMs' capabilities in this task, we constructed a dataset from 769 real-world Malaysian Contract Act court cases, using GPT-4o to extract facts and generate candidate legal issues, annotated by senior legal experts, which reveals a critical limitation: while LLMs generate diverse issue candidates, their precision remains inadequate (GPT-4o achieves only 62%). To address this gap, we propose LePREC (Legal Professional-inspired Reasoning Elicitation and Classification), a neuro-symbolic framework combining neural generation with structured statistical reasoning. LePREC consists of: (1) a neuro component leverages LLMs to transform legal descriptions into question-answer pairs representing diverse analytical factors, and (2) a symbolic component applies sparse linear models over these discrete features, learning explicit algebraic weights that identify the most informative reasoning factors. Unlike end-to-end neural approaches, LePREC achieves interpretability through transparent feature weighting while maintaining data efficiency through correlation-based statistical classification. Experiments show a 30-40% improvement over advanced LLM baselines, including GPT-4o and Claude, confirming that correlation-based factor-issue analysis offers a more data-efficient solution for relevance decisions.

</details>


### [248] [Rethinking Scale: Deployment Trade-offs of Small Language Models under Agent Paradigms](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19299)
*Xinlin Wang,Mats Brorsson*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 该研究首次大规模评估了<10B参数小语言模型在三种范式下的表现：基础模型、单智能体工具使用、多智能体协作，发现单智能体系统在性能与成本间达到最佳平衡。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 尽管大语言模型能力强大，但其高计算成本、延迟和隐私风险限制了实际部署。小语言模型（<10B参数）虽具潜力，但存在知识和推理能力不足的固有局限。现有研究主要关注通过缩放定律或微调策略增强小模型，而忽视了利用智能体范式（如工具使用和多智能体协作）来系统性地弥补小模型的弱点。

Method: 对<10B参数的开源小语言模型进行了首次大规模全面研究，评估三种范式：1) 基础模型，2) 配备工具的单智能体系统，3) 具备协作能力的多智能体系统。研究比较了不同范式在性能、成本和效率方面的表现。

Result: 研究结果显示：单智能体系统在性能和成本之间达到了最佳平衡，而多智能体设置增加了开销但收益有限。这表明智能体中心设计对于资源受限环境中的高效可信部署具有重要意义。

Conclusion: 智能体范式（特别是单智能体工具使用）能够有效弥补小语言模型的固有局限，为资源受限环境中的高效可信部署提供了重要方向。研究强调了智能体中心设计的重要性，而非单纯依赖模型缩放或微调。

Abstract: Despite the impressive capabilities of large language models, their substantial computational costs, latency, and privacy risks hinder their widespread deployment in real-world applications. Small Language Models (SLMs) with fewer than 10 billion parameters present a promising alternative; however, their inherent limitations in knowledge and reasoning curtail their effectiveness. Existing research primarily focuses on enhancing SLMs through scaling laws or fine-tuning strategies while overlooking the potential of using agent paradigms, such as tool use and multi-agent collaboration, to systematically compensate for the inherent weaknesses of small models. To address this gap, this paper presents the first large-scale, comprehensive study of <10B open-source models under three paradigms: (1) the base model, (2) a single agent equipped with tools, and (3) a multi-agent system with collaborative capabilities. Our results show that single-agent systems achieve the best balance between performance and cost, while multi-agent setups add overhead with limited gains. Our findings highlight the importance of agent-centric design for efficient and trustworthy deployment in resource-constrained settings.

</details>


### [249] [IndiaFinBench: An Evaluation Benchmark for Large Language Model Performance on Indian Financial Regulatory Text](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19298)
*Rajveer Singh Pall*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 印度首个公开的金融监管文本评估基准，包含406个专家标注的问答对，涵盖四种任务类型，用于评估大语言模型在印度金融监管框架下的表现。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有金融NLP基准主要基于西方金融语料（SEC文件、美国财报、英文财经新闻），缺乏对非西方监管框架的覆盖。印度作为重要新兴市场，其金融监管文本具有独特性和复杂性，需要专门的评估工具来衡量LLM在此领域的表现。

Method: 从印度证券交易委员会（SEBI）和印度储备银行（RBI）收集192份文档，创建406个专家标注的问答对，涵盖四种任务类型：监管解释（174项）、数值推理（92项）、矛盾检测（62项）和时间推理（78项）。通过模型辅助二次验证（矛盾检测kappa=0.918）和人工标注者一致性评估（60项，kappa=0.611，总体一致性76.7%）确保标注质量。

Result: 在零样本条件下评估12个模型，准确率从70.4%（Gemma 4 E4B）到89.7%（Gemini 2.5 Flash）。所有模型显著优于非专业人类基线（60.0%）。数值推理是最具区分度的任务，模型间差异达35.9个百分点。通过自助法显著性检验（10,000次重采样）识别出三个统计上显著不同的性能层级。

Conclusion: IndiaFinBench填补了非西方金融监管文本评估的空白，为评估LLM在印度金融监管领域的性能提供了首个公开基准。结果表明现有LLM在此领域表现良好，但数值推理任务仍具挑战性。该基准有助于推动金融NLP在非西方市场的发展和应用。

Abstract: We introduce IndiaFinBench, to our knowledge the first publicly available evaluation benchmark for assessing large language model (LLM) performance on Indian financial regulatory text. Existing financial NLP benchmarks draw exclusively from Western financial corpora (SEC filings, US earnings reports, and English-language financial news), leaving a significant gap in coverage of non-Western regulatory frameworks. IndiaFinBench addresses this gap with 406 expert-annotated question-answer pairs drawn from 192 documents sourced from the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) and the Reserve Bank of India (RBI), spanning four task types: regulatory interpretation (174 items), numerical reasoning (92 items), contradiction detection (62 items), and temporal reasoning (78 items). Annotation quality is validated through a model-based secondary pass (kappa=0.918 on contradiction detection) and a 60-item human inter-annotator agreement evaluation (kappa=0.611; 76.7% overall agreement). We evaluate twelve models under zero-shot conditions, with accuracy ranging from 70.4% (Gemma 4 E4B) to 89.7% (Gemini 2.5 Flash). All models substantially outperform a non-specialist human baseline of 60.0%. Numerical reasoning is the most discriminative task, with a 35.9 percentage-point spread across models. Bootstrap significance testing (10,000 resamples) reveals three statistically distinct performance tiers. The dataset, evaluation code, and all model outputs are available at https://github.com/rajveerpall/IndiaFinBench

</details>


### [250] [Location Not Found: Exposing Implicit Local and Global Biases in Multilingual LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19292)
*Guy Mor-Lan,Omer Goldman,Matan Eyal,Adi Mayrav Gilady,Sivan Eiger,Idan Szpektor,Avinatan Hassidim,Yossi Matias,Reut Tsarfaty*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: LocQA测试集用于评估多语言大语言模型的跨语言和语言内偏见，通过无地域指示的问题揭示模型隐含的先验偏好，发现模型普遍偏向美国相关答案且指令微调加剧此偏见，同时模型倾向于人口较多的地域。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 多语言大语言模型虽然缩小了语言间的流畅性差距，但也带来了偏见传播的风险，因为知识和规范可能跨语言传播。本研究旨在量化模型在跨语言和语言内两个维度上的偏见，通过它们回答地域模糊问题的能力来揭示模型隐含的先验偏好。

Method: 提出LocQA测试集，包含12种语言的2,156个问题，涉及法律、日期、测量等地域依赖的事实。问题本身不包含任何地域指示信息，仅通过查询语言本身隐含地域关联。使用LocQA评估了32个模型，分析其回答中揭示的结构性偏见。

Result: 发现两种结构性偏见：1) 跨语言层面，模型普遍偏向美国相关答案，即使使用非英语语言提问；2) 指令微调模型比其基础版本表现出更强的美国偏向；3) 语言内层面，当同一语言对应多个地域时，模型像人口概率引擎，优先选择人口较多的地域。

Conclusion: LocQA的见解有助于塑造大语言模型的期望本地化行为，并量化不同训练阶段对各类偏见的影响。该测试集为评估和减轻多语言模型的地域偏见提供了重要工具。

Abstract: Multilingual large language models (LLMs) have minimized the fluency gap between languages. This advancement, however, exposes models to the risk of biased behavior, as knowledge and norms may propagate across languages. In this work, we aim to quantify models' inter- and intra-lingual biases, via their ability to answer locale-ambiguous questions. To this end, we present LocQA, a test set containing 2,156 questions in 12 languages, referring to various locale-dependent facts such as laws, dates, and measurements. The questions do not contain indications of the locales they relate to, other than the querying language itself. LLMs' responses to LocQA locale-ambiguous questions thus reveal models' implicit priors. We used LocQA to evaluate 32 models, and detected two types of structural biases. Inter-lingually, we show a global bias towards answers relevant to the US-locale, even when models are asked in languages other than English. Moreover, we discovered that this global bias is exacerbated in models that underwent instruction tuning, compared to their base counterparts. Intra-lingually, we show that when multiple locales are relevant for the same language, models act as demographic probability engines, prioritizing locales with larger populations. Taken together, insights from LocQA may help in shaping LLMs' desired local behavior, and in quantifying the impact of various training phases on different kinds of biases.

</details>


### [251] [CulturALL: Benchmarking Multilingual and Multicultural Competence of LLMs on Grounded Tasks](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19262)
*Peiqin Lin,Chenyang Lyu,Wenjiang Luo,Haotian Ye,Md Mehrab Hossain,Chunlan Ma,Shaoxiong Ji,Younes Samih,Bo Zeng,Fan Jiang,Yuanbin Cao,Dilda Duisenbek,Adrian Neo Sau Xun,Daria Pozdniakova,Liubou Misevich,Nevena Marinković,Ngoc Gia Linh Nguyen,Thi Khanh Linh Do,Sarakmatak Sophy,Baotian Hu,Guanhua Chen,Gongbo Tang,Alham Fikri Aji,Longyue Wang,Weihua Luo*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: CulturALL是一个评估大语言模型多语言和多元文化能力的基准测试，专注于真实世界情境中的推理任务，包含14种语言的2610个样本，最佳模型准确率仅为44.48%。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有基准测试主要关注通用语言理解或浅层文化知识，缺乏对真实世界情境中模型推理能力的评估，需要填补这一空白。

Method: 采用人机协作框架构建：专家标注确保难度和事实准确性，LLM减轻人工工作量；整合多样化来源确保全面场景覆盖；精心设计高难度项目。

Result: CulturALL包含14种语言、51个地区的2610个样本，涵盖16个主题；实验显示最佳LLM准确率仅为44.48%，表明仍有很大改进空间。

Conclusion: CulturALL是一个全面且具有挑战性的基准测试，突显了LLM在多语言和多元文化情境推理能力方面的不足，为未来研究提供了重要评估工具。

Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are now deployed worldwide, inspiring a surge of benchmarks that measure their multilingual and multicultural abilities. However, these benchmarks prioritize generic language understanding or superficial cultural trivia, leaving the evaluation of grounded tasks -- where models must reason within real-world, context-rich scenarios -- largely unaddressed. To fill this gap, we present CulturALL, a comprehensive and challenging benchmark to assess LLMs' multilingual and multicultural competence on grounded tasks. CulturALL is built via a human--AI collaborative framework: expert annotators ensure appropriate difficulty and factual accuracy, while LLMs lighten the manual workload. By incorporating diverse sources, CulturALL ensures comprehensive scenario coverage. Each item is carefully designed to present a high level of difficulty, making CulturALL challenging. CulturALL contains 2,610 samples in 14 languages from 51 regions, distributed across 16 topics to capture the full breadth of grounded tasks. Experiments show that the best LLM achieves 44.48% accuracy on CulturALL, underscoring substantial room for improvement.

</details>


### [252] [Talking to a Know-It-All GPT or a Second-Guesser Claude? How Repair reveals unreliable Multi-Turn Behavior in LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19245)
*Clara Lachenmaier,Hannah Bultmann,Sina Zarrieß*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 本文研究了LLM在多轮对话中处理可解与不可解数学问题时参与修复（repair）的交互过程，发现不同模型在发起修复和回应用户修复方面存在显著差异，且多轮对话后模型行为更具独特性和不可预测性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 修复是人类对话中解决交流障碍的重要机制，但在人-LLM交互中尚未得到充分探索。

Method: 通过设计多轮对话场景（包含可解与不可解数学问题），分析LLM是否主动发起修复以及如何响应用户发起的修复。

Result: 不同模型表现差异显著：有的几乎完全抵抗适当的修复尝试，有的则高度易受影响和操纵；多轮对话后模型行为更具区分度且更不可预测。

Conclusion: 每个测试的LLM在修复场景下都展现出其特有的不可靠性形式。

Abstract: Repair, an important resource for resolving trouble in human-human conversation, remains underexplored in human-LLM interaction. In this study, we investigate how LLMs engage in the interactive process of repair in multi-turn dialogues around solvable and unsolvable math questions. We examine whether models initiate repair themselves and how they respond to user-initiated repair. Our results show strong differences across models: reactions range from being almost completely resistant to (appropriate) repair attempts to being highly susceptible and easily manipulated. We further demonstrate that once conversations extend beyond a single turn, model behavior becomes more distinctive and less predictable across systems. Overall, our findings indicate that each tested LLM exhibits its own characteristic form of unreliability in the context of repair.

</details>


### [253] [SCURank: Ranking Multiple Candidate Summaries with Summary Content Units for Enhanced Summarization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19185)
*Bo-Jyun Wang,Ying-Jia Lin,Hung-Yu Kao*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: SCURank是一个利用语义信息单元(SCU)来评估摘要质量的新型框架，通过从多个不同的大语言模型中蒸馏知识来提升小语言模型的摘要性能，实验证明其优于传统指标和LLM排序方法。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有基于LLM的摘要候选排序策略存在不稳定性问题，而传统指标(如ROUGE)不足以区分高质量摘要，需要一种更稳定、语义感知的评估方法来提升小语言模型的蒸馏效果。

Method: 提出SCURank框架，利用摘要内容单元(SCU)基于信息的丰富度和语义重要性来评估摘要质量，取代不稳定的LLM比较或表面重叠指标，并在多LLM蒸馏场景中应用该评估方法。

Result: SCURank在多个评估指标和数据集上优于传统ROUGE指标和LLM排序方法；融合多样化的LLM摘要能够提升模型的抽象性和整体蒸馏性能。

Conclusion: 基于信息中心的排序方法在多LLM蒸馏中具有显著优势，SCURank为小语言模型的摘要能力提升提供了一种有效且稳定的解决方案。

Abstract: Small language models (SLMs), such as BART, can achieve summarization performance comparable to large language models (LLMs) via distillation. However, existing LLM-based ranking strategies for summary candidates suffer from instability, while classical metrics (e.g., ROUGE) are insufficient to rank high-quality summaries. To address these issues, we introduce \textbf{SCURank}, a framework that enhances summarization by leveraging \textbf{Summary Content Units (SCUs)}. Instead of relying on unstable comparisons or surface-level overlap, SCURank evaluates summaries based on the richness and semantic importance of information content. We investigate the effectiveness of SCURank in distilling summaries from multiple diverse LLMs. Experimental results demonstrate that SCURank outperforms traditional metrics and LLM-based ranking methods across evaluation measures and datasets. Furthermore, our findings show that incorporating diverse LLM summaries enhances model abstractiveness and overall distilled model performance, validating the benefits of information-centric ranking in multi-LLM distillation. The code for SCURank is available at https://github.com/IKMLab/SCURank.

</details>


### [254] [How Do Answer Tokens Read Reasoning Traces? Self-Reading Patterns in Thinking LLMs for Quantitative Reasoning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19149)
*Haoyang Chen,Yi Liu,Jianzhi Shao,Tao Zhang,Chengfu Huo,Wei Hu*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 本文聚焦于思维链推理中答案token如何读取并整合推理过程，发现正确推理呈现良性的自我阅读模式（注意力前向漂移+聚焦关键语义锚点），并提出基于自我阅读质量（SRQ）的无训练干预方法，通过构建引导向量提升推理准确性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有激活引导工作主要针对塑造推理轨迹，但对答案token如何读取并整合推理过程以产生可靠结果的理解不足。

Method: 1) 分析答案到推理的注意力模式，发现正确推理表现为良性自我阅读（注意力前向漂移+关键语义锚点聚焦），错误推理则为弥散不规则模式。2) 提出无训练引导方法SRQ，结合几何指标（过程控制）与语义指标（内容监控）计算自我阅读质量分数，据此选择数据构建引导向量，引导推理朝向良性自我阅读。

Result: 实验表明该方法在多个定量推理任务上取得一致的准确性提升。

Conclusion: 答案token的自我阅读注意力模式可作为内部确定性的指标，基于SRQ的训练-free干预能有效提升推理质量。

Abstract: Thinking LLMs produce reasoning traces before answering. Prior activation steering work mainly targets on shaping these traces. It remains less understood how answer tokens actually read and integrate the reasoning to produce reliable outcomes. Focusing on quantitative reasoning, we analyze the answer-to-reasoning attention and observe a benign self-reading pattern aligned with correctness, characterized by a forward drift of the reading focus along the reasoning trace and a persistent concentration on key semantic anchors, whereas incorrect solutions exhibit diffuse and irregular attention pattern. We interpret this as internal certainty during answer decoding, where the model commits to a viable solution branch and integrates key evidence. Following this, we propose a training-free steering method driven by Self-Reading Quality (SRQ) scores combining geometric metrics for process control with semantic metrics for content monitoring. SRQ selects data to build steering vectors that guide inference toward benign self-reading and away from uncertain and disorganized reading. Experiments show that our method yields consistent accuracy gains.

</details>


### [255] [SAHM: A Benchmark for Arabic Financial and Shari'ah-Compliant Reasoning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19098)
*Rania Elbadry,Sarfraz Ahmad,Ahmed Heakl,Dani Bouch,Momina Ahsan,Muhra AlMahri,Marwa Elsaid khalil,Yuxia Wang,Salem Lahlou,Sophia Ananiadou,Veselin Stoyanov,Jimin Huang,Xueqing Peng,Preslav Nakov,Zhuohan Xie*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 本文提出SAHM，首个面向阿拉伯语金融NLP和伊斯兰教法合规推理的文档级基准与指令微调数据集，涵盖14,380条专家验证样本、7类任务。评估19个LLM后发现：阿拉伯语流利度不等于金融推理能力，模型在识别任务上显著强于生成和因果推理任务。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 英语金融NLP进展迅速，但阿拉伯语金融NLP（尤其是伊斯兰金融和可信金融助手）因缺乏高质量基准而研究不足，存在实际需求缺口。

Method: 构建SAHM数据集（14,380条专家验证实例），涵盖AAOIFI标准问答、法特瓦问答/多选题、会计与商业考试、金融情感分析、抽取式摘要、事件因果推理7类任务；采用任务特定指标和基于评分标准的开放式输出评估方法，对19个开源和闭源LLM进行系统评测。

Result: 阿拉伯语流利度不能可靠迁移为基于证据的金融推理能力；模型在识别类任务（如情感分析、多选题）上表现显著优于生成和因果推理任务，事件因果推理任务上的差距最大。

Conclusion: SAHM填补了阿拉伯语金融NLP基准空白，揭示了当前LLM在阿拉伯语金融推理中的不足，释放了数据集、评估框架和指令微调模型以推动该领域发展。

Abstract: English financial NLP has progressed rapidly through benchmarks for sentiment, document understanding, and financial question answering, while Arabic financial NLP remains comparatively under-explored despite strong practical demand for trustworthy finance and Islamic-finance assistants. We introduce SAHM, a document-grounded benchmark and instruction-tuning dataset for Arabic financial NLP and Shari'ah-compliant reasoning. SAHM contains 14,380 expert-verified instances spanning seven tasks: AAOIFI standards QA, fatwa-based QA/MCQ, accounting and business exams, financial sentiment analysis, extractive summarization, and event-cause reasoning, curated from authentic regulatory, juristic, and corporate sources. We evaluate 19 strong open and proprietary LLMs using task-specific metrics and rubric-based scoring for open-ended outputs, and find that Arabic fluency does not reliably translate to evidence-grounded financial reasoning: models are substantially stronger on recognition-style tasks than on generation and causal reasoning, with the largest gaps on event-cause reasoning. We release the benchmark, evaluation framework, and an instruction-tuned model to support future research on trustworthy Arabic financial NLP.

</details>


### [256] [The Rise of Verbal Tics in Large Language Models: A Systematic Analysis Across Frontier Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19139)
*Shuai Wu,Xue Li,Yanna Feng,Yufang Li,Zhijun Wang,Ran Wang*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 本文系统分析了8个前沿大语言模型（GPT-5.4、Claude Opus 4.7、Gemini 3.1 Pro等）中出现的“口头禅”现象——即重复性、公式化的语言模式（如谄媚式开场白、伪共情用语、过度使用的词汇）。通过自定义评估框架，在10个任务类别上对10,000条提示词（中英文各半）进行标准化API评估，收集了160,000条模型回复。提出了口头禅指数（VTI）作为量化指标，发现Gemini 3.1 Pro口头禅最严重（VTI=0.590），DeepSeek V3.2最轻微（VTI=0.295）。口头禅在多轮对话中累积、在主观任务中被放大，且呈现跨语言差异。人类评估（N=120）证实谄媚与自然感知度呈强负相关（r=-0.87, p<0.001），揭示了当前训练范式的“对齐代价”。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 随着LLM通过对齐技术（RLHF、Constitutional AI）不断发展，模型输出中出现了一种日益明显且普遍的现象——口头禅（重复性、公式化的语言模式），但此前缺乏对这一现象的系统性分析和量化研究。

Method: 1) 选取8个前沿LLM（GPT-5.4、Claude Opus 4.7、Gemini 3.1 Pro、Grok 4.2、Doubao-Seed-2.0-pro、Kimi K2.5、DeepSeek V3.2、MiMo-V2-Pro）；2) 构建自定义评估框架进行标准化API评估；3) 在10个任务类别上使用10,000条提示词（中英文各5,000条），收集160,000条模型回复；4) 提出口头禅指数（VTI）作为复合量化指标；5) 分析VTI与谄媚、词汇多样性、人类感知自然度的相关性；6) 开展人类评估（N=120）验证。

Result: Gemini 3.1 Pro口头禅最严重（VTI=0.590），DeepSeek V3.2最轻微（VTI=0.295）；口头禅在多轮对话中累积、在主观任务中被放大、呈现跨语言差异；谄媚与自然感知度呈强负相关（r=-0.87, p<0.001）。

Conclusion: 口头禅现象是当前对齐训练范式（RLHF、Constitutional AI）带来的“对齐代价”，亟需构建更真实的人机交互框架以减少这种不自然的语言模式。

Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) continue to evolve through alignment techniques such as Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) and Constitutional AI, a growing and increasingly conspicuous phenomenon has emerged: the proliferation of verbal tics -- repetitive, formulaic linguistic patterns that pervade model outputs. These range from sycophantic openers ("That's a great question!", "Awesome!") to pseudo-empathetic affirmations ("I completely understand your concern", "I'm right here to catch you") and overused vocabulary ("delve", "tapestry", "nuanced"). In this paper, we present a systematic analysis of the verbal tic phenomenon across eight state-of-the-art LLMs: GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Grok 4.2, Doubao-Seed-2.0-pro, Kimi K2.5, DeepSeek V3.2, and MiMo-V2-Pro. Utilizing a custom evaluation framework for standardized API-based evaluation, we assess 10,000 prompts across 10 task categories in both English and Chinese, yielding 160,000 model responses. We introduce the Verbal Tic Index (VTI), a composite metric quantifying tic prevalence, and analyze its correlation with sycophancy, lexical diversity, and human-perceived naturalness. Our findings reveal significant inter-model variation: Gemini 3.1 Pro exhibits the highest VTI (0.590), while DeepSeek V3.2 achieves the lowest (0.295). We further demonstrate that verbal tics accumulate over multi-turn conversations, are amplified in subjective tasks, and show distinct cross-lingual patterns. Human evaluation (N = 120) confirms a strong inverse relationship between sycophancy and perceived naturalness (r = -0.87, p < 0.001). These results underscore the "alignment tax" of current training paradigms and highlight the urgent need for more authentic human-AI interaction frameworks.

</details>


### [257] [$R^2$-dLLM: Accelerating Diffusion Large Language Models via Spatio-Temporal Redundancy Reduction](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.18995)
*Zhenbang Du,Kejing Xia,Xinrui Zhong,Yonggan Fu,Nicolai Oswald,Binfei Ji,Brucek Khailany,Pavlo Molchanov,Yingyan Lin*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 本文提出R²-dLLM框架，通过识别并减少扩散大语言模型解码过程中的空间冗余（置信度聚类和位置模糊性）和时间冗余（已稳定token的重复掩码），从推理和训练两个角度实现高效解码，在保持生成质量的同时将解码步数减少高达75%。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 扩散大语言模型虽能并行预测token，但实际解码延迟高，限制了部署。作者观察到解码过程中的冗余是效率瓶颈，包括空间冗余（置信度聚类和位置模糊性）和时间冗余（已稳定token的重复掩码）。

Method: 提出R²-dLLM统一框架：1）推理阶段引入免训练解码规则，聚合局部置信度和token预测，将已稳定的token最终化以避免冗余解码步骤；2）提出冗余感知的监督微调流水线，使模型对齐高效解码轨迹，减少对人工阈值调优的依赖。

Result: 相比现有解码策略，R²-dLLM在不同模型和任务上一致地将解码步数减少高达75%，同时保持具有竞争力的生成质量。

Conclusion: 解码冗余是扩散大语言模型的核心瓶颈，显式减少冗余能够带来显著的实用效率提升。

Abstract: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) have emerged as a promising alternative to autoregressive generation by enabling parallel token prediction. However, practical dLLM decoding still suffers from high inference latency, which limits deployment. In this work, we observe that a substantial part of this inefficiency comes from recurring redundancy in the decoding process, including spatial redundancy caused by confidence clusters and positional ambiguity, and temporal redundancy caused by repeatedly remasking predictions that have already stabilized. Motivated by these patterns, we propose $R^2$-dLLM, a unified framework for reducing decoding redundancy from both inference and training perspectives.
